records.ety: [L. importare to bring in, to occasion, to cause; pref. im- in + portare to bear. Sense 3 comes through F. importer, from the Latin. See Port demeanor.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Imported; p. pr. & vb. n. Importing.]

defs: To bring in from abroad; to introduce from without; especially, to bring (wares or merchandise) into a place or country from a foreign country, in the transactions of commerce; -- opposed to export. We import teas from China, coffee from Brazil, etc.
To carry or include, as meaning or intention; to imply; to signify.
quotes: Every petition . . . doth . . . always import a multitude of speakers together.

defs: To be of importance or consequence to; to have a bearing on; to concern.
quotes: I have a motion much imports your good.
If I endure it, what imports it you?

defs: To signify; to purport; to be of moment.
Merchandise imported, or brought into a country from without its boundaries; -- generally in the plural, opposed to exports.
quotes: I take the imports from, and not the exports to, these conquests, as the measure of these advantages which we derived from them.

defs: That which a word, phrase, or document contains as its signification or intention or interpretation of a word, action, event, and the like.

quotes: Most serious design, and the great import.
